summarization.a: The scatter plot shows the relationship between vacuum pressure (in Pa) and etching per cycle (in Å/cycle) at different speeds (60 mm/s, 90 mm/s, and 120 mm/s).

data_extraction.a:
| Vacuum pressure (Pa) | Etching per cycle (Å/cycle) 60mm/s |Etching per cycle (Å/cycle) 90mm/s |Etching per cycle (Å/cycle) 120mm/s |
|---|---|---|---|
|-200 | 0.37 |0.25|0.13|
|-150 | 0.40 |0.30|0.20|
|-100 | 0.40 |0.33|0.22|
|-50 | 0.40 |0.33|0.26|

a.question: How would the trend look like in the ideal situation?
a.answer: a	In the ideal case the vacuum pressure between the sample and the table does not have an influence on the trend. I.e. a straightline should be observed.

a.question: What could be the reason for the decrease in EPC for lower vacuum pressure, especially for a velocity of 120 mm/s?
a.answer: a	It could be that the process gases are pumped away during the dosing, due to the low vacuum pressure.

a.question: What are the saturated EPCs for 60 mm/s and 90 mm/s?
What type of trend is observed for 120 mm/s?
